exercises: SIMPLE_BACKUP_SUFFIX sets the backup suffix when --backup makes a simple backup
expected: The existing destination is backed up using the suffix from SIMPLE_BACKUP_SUFFIX.

exercises: -S/--suffix overrides the usual backup suffix, including SIMPLE_BACKUP_SUFFIX
expected: The backup uses the suffix supplied by -S, not the environment suffix.

exercises: VERSION_CONTROL=none: documented to never make backups even if --backup is given
expected: No simple or numbered backup file is created.

exercises: VERSION_CONTROL=off alias for none: documented to never make backups even if --backup is given
expected: No simple or numbered backup file is created.
The old destination is saved as the first numbered backup.
The old destination is saved as the first numbered backup.

exercises: VERSION_CONTROL=existing: simple backup when no numbered backups exist
expected: With no numbered backup already present, the old destination is saved as a simple backup.

exercises: VERSION_CONTROL=existing: numbered backup if numbered backups exist
expected: With an existing numbered backup, the old destination is saved as the next numbered backup.

exercises: VERSION_CONTROL=nil alias for existing: numbered backup if numbered backups exist
expected: With an existing numbered backup, the old destination is saved as the next numbered backup.

exercises: VERSION_CONTROL=simple always makes simple backups, even when numbered backups exist
expected: The old destination is saved as a simple backup.

exercises: VERSION_CONTROL=never alias for simple: always make simple backups
expected: The old destination is saved as a simple backup.

exercises: Version control method may be selected via --backup option or VERSION_CONTROL; explicit --backup=CONTROL selects simple over env numbered
expected: The explicit --backup=simple control creates a simple backup despite VERSION_CONTROL=numbered.

exercises: --backup is the option that makes backups; VERSION_CONTROL only selects the method
expected: No backup file is created when --backup is absent, even with VERSION_CONTROL set.

exercises: VERSION_CONTROL is documented to accept only the listed control values
expected: An unlisted VERSION_CONTROL value is rejected with nonzero exit status.

exercises: --backup[=CONTROL] is documented with a finite set of CONTROL values
expected: An unlisted --backup control value is rejected with nonzero exit status.

exercises: --update=none-fail: skipped existing destination files are diagnosed and induce a failure
expected: Moving onto an existing destination with --update=none-fail exits nonzero.
An unlisted --update argument is rejected with nonzero exit status.
